frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: Partial whitening of correlated task variables across neural population modes.

frameworks.name: neural population coding
frameworks.one_line: Population modes, not single neurons, as the unit of representation.

frameworks.name: sequential neural dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Random sequential activity used as a temporal scaffold for time-dependent encoding.

core_question: How can multiple correlated task variables be represented simultaneously in cortex without interference and updated through time?
key_assumptions: Population modes are the relevant encoding unit, not individual neurons
Task variables can be correlated yet must be jointly decodable
Sequential dynamics can serve as a temporal substrate for stable encoding
manipulations: Multiple interrelated task variables varied within a dynamic behavioural task
Task-variable correlations change throughout the trial
key_findings: Highly correlated task variables map onto less-correlated neural population modes
Pairs of neurons show a spectrum of signal correlations
Different variables are encoded with different signal-to-noise levels (partial whitening)
The encoding function is multiplexed with sequential neural dynamics and tracks changing task-variable correlations
interpretations: Cortex implements an efficient, partially-whitened population code at the level of modes
Random sequential dynamics provide a simple temporal scaffold for time-dependent encoding
